A symptom-first field guide

Take the Chunk.

RoUn’s Engines can be installed as complete runtime systems. Every chunk is also open for individual use. This page reorganizes them by the failures they are meant to prevent, so you can inspect the exact guardrail, copy the full source text, and use only what your bot needs.

Browse by Symptom Search All Chunks Use Full Engines ↗

Why do feature engines contain so many guardrails?

RoUn’s Engines were built to do specific things: move the world, control discovery, manage NPC gravity, preserve spatial and bodily continuity, sustain character-specific relationship behavior, and keep scenes playable. But those functions cannot survive on positive instructions alone.

To move a world without random escalation, an engine must stop the model from activating every available plot. To produce distinct relationship behavior, it must stop different characters from collapsing into the same dominant, protective, possessive archetype. To preserve a mystery, it must control who knows what and when an answer becomes accessible. To maintain continuity, it must prevent rooms, objects, injuries, bodies, and combat advantages from resetting whenever the prose moves on.

The guardrails are there because the engine could not achieve its primary function without targeting the places where models repeatedly fail. Those same chunks may therefore be useful outside the full engine. Install the entire system, or take only the guardrail in front of you. They are open for both.
